feed.title: Radius Red Blog
feed.subtitle: Engineering notes, release updates, and insights from the Radius Red team.

entry.title: Textbook Trend-Following on the Wrong Instrument — Why Donchian Channels Failed on Gold
entry.summary: Gold is supposed to trend. Donchian channels are built for trends. So why did both channel variants fail to deliver alpha during the US session?

entry.title: When the best edge rationale is not enough
entry.summary: The IBS mean-reversion strategy had the strongest academic case of anything we tested. Discovery Sharpe 2.65. Validation Sharpe -0.62. This is the story of why.

entry.title: The First US-Session Gap — Spike-Fade and the Cost of Naive Breakouts
entry.summary: Our mean-reversion portfolio was silent during the US session. We tested breakout strategies to fill the gap. The spike-fade pattern taught us why it failed.

entry.title: Why a winning Gold momentum idea failed on Brent and Silver
entry.summary: The same strategy archetype that fit Gold broke on Brent crude and Silver. The lesson was about intraday microstructure, not commodities in general.

entry.title: tradedesk v1.0.1 Fixes a Startup Retry Edge Case
entry.summary: A short release note on the tradedesk v1.0.1 patch release, covering the IG chart subscription retry fix and two streamer cleanups.

entry.title: What an open-source 1.0.0 looks like when an upstream API moves
entry.summary: Why tradedesk 1.0.0 shipped now, what changed in IG streaming support, and how a release workflow bug became part of the work.

entry.title: Phase 5 Quant Loop: From Research Consensus to Implementation Reality
entry.summary: An inside look at the checks between a promising backtest and something that is actually safe enough to earn a DEMO slot.

entry.title: When Research Says Go but Implementation Says Wait
entry.summary: A process-focused look at how we handle promising research, implementation reality checks, and explicit decision gates.
